title: Review of the SME Banking Undertakings 2002
body: "In 2014, the CMA reviewed the SME Banking Undertakings 2002 and decided in 2016 to release all but 4 provisions in the Undertakings. These 4 provisions prohibit 8 designated banks from compelling an SME customer to open or maintain a business current account as a condition of accessing business loans or deposit accounts (the ‘Limitation on Bundling Provisions’).  The purpose of this review is to determine whether, as a result of any change of circumstances, the Limitation on Bundling Provisions are no longer appropriate and need to be varied, superseded or released.  The CMA consulted on:   potential changes of circumstances that may mean that the Limitation on Bundling Provisions are no longer appropriate if such changes are identified, whether they mean that the Limitation on Bundling Provisions should be varied, superseded or released in the case of identifying the need to vary the Limitation on Bundling Provisions, what are the proposed changes that could be made   "
